herbs.herb_desc: The capsule of the Aesculus hippocastanum, also known as the horse chestnut tree, is a spiky, green fruit that contains 2-4 seeds. As the seeds mature, the capsule dries and splits open, releasing the seeds, which are encased in a brown, stony coat. The capsule itself remains on the tree through the winter, eventually falling off as new growth begins in the spring.

The capsule of Allium sativum, commonly known as garlic, is a dry, dehiscent fruit that splits open to release its seeds. It is typically brown or tan in color and has a characteristic, papery texture. The capsule is an important component of the garlic plant, serving as a means of seed dispersal and contributing to the plant's reproductive cycle.
